acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Construction of a frame with seq=1 and prev set (or seq>1 without prev) is impossible without a fallible conversion.
- [ ] `verify_event_frame` takes typed frame and no longer does prev/seq validation internally.
- [ ] Existing framing sources updated to use constructors; tests updated accordingly.
description:
**Problem**
`EventFrameV1` allows impossible combinations of `origin_seq` and `prev_sha256` (e.g., seq=1 with prev set), and these are rejected only at runtime in `verify_event_frame`. This is a structural invariant that should be expressed in the type system so callers can’t construct invalid frames.

Key references:
- `crates/beads-core/src/event.rs:2500` — runtime prev/seq checks in `verify_event_frame`.
- `crates/beads-core/src/event.rs:150` — `EventFrameV1` definition.

Severity: low‑level framing invariants are enforced only by convention; easy to break when creating new frame sources.
design:
**Design**
Add typestated frame variants to encode the prev/seq relation.

Option A (sum types):
- `enum EventFrame { Genesis(GenesisFrame), WithPrev(NonGenesisFrame) }`.
- `GenesisFrame` has `origin_seq = Seq1::from_u64(1)` and `prev_sha256 = None`.
- `NonGenesisFrame` carries `origin_seq > 1` and `prev_sha256 = Some`.

Option B (phantom state):
- `EventFrameV1<HasPrev>` with `HasPrev = PrevRequired | PrevForbidden` and constructors enforcing seq rules.

Migration:
- Keep wire parsing into raw struct, then convert into typed frame with a fallible conversion.
- Update `verify_event_frame` to accept only typed frame; runtime prev/seq checks become unreachable.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: Rendering should remain in command files; avoid extracting rendering into shared helpers.

notes.author: darin@darinsmcstudio2.lan
notes.content: Rendering should remain in command files; avoid extracting rendering into shared helpers.

title: Encode EventFrame prev/seq invariants in types

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Snapshot captures included watermarks and shard contents from a single coordinator tick.
- [ ] Snapshot object is immutable once created and safe to pass to worker thread.
- [ ] Export worker consumes only snapshot data (no live reads during I/O).
- [ ] Snapshot includes metadata needed for manifest/meta construction without touching live state.
description:
**Problem**
Checkpoint export requires a snapshot barrier that freezes the state and included watermarks together. Without a typed barrier, the coordinator can accidentally mix shards from different logical times or claim watermarks that aren’t represented in the exported content.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §13.8 (checkpoint snapshot barrier and consistency rule)
- Checkpoint scheduling in §13.7 and export workflow in §13.8

**Files:** src/git/checkpoint/types.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/export.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
design:
**Design**
- Introduce an immutable CheckpointSnapshot struct: { group_id, policy_hash, roster_hash, included_watermarks (durable), included_heads?, shard_payloads/dirty_shards, created_at_ms }.
- The coordinator thread captures the snapshot under a single lock so shard contents and watermarks correspond to the same logical point in time.
- Make the snapshot owned/immutable (e.g., Arc or owned values), so the worker thread cannot observe concurrent mutations.
- Enforce a check: included_watermarks must not exceed the max seq represented by shard_payloads; if it would, delay snapshot or recompute.
title: Phase 6: CheckpointSnapshot barrier type
